query: how does the middleware chain work
note: Next()/handlers index advance live in context.go; combineHandlers + Use that build the chain live in routergroup.go. Hand-judged.

query: how are panics recovered during request handling
note: Recovery middleware that catches panics and writes a 500. Single, unambiguous gold.

query: how does request body binding and validation work
note: Binding interface + Default() content-type dispatch. The concrete binders are siblings; binding.go is the canonical entry point.

query: how does the radix tree route matching work
note: The httprouter-derived radix tree (addRoute/getValue) lives entirely in tree.go. Single gold.

query: how are accumulated request errors collected and reported
note: Error / errorMsgs type + c.Error() accumulation. context.go holds Error() but the error model is errors.go. Hand-judged primary.

query: how do I register a route handler for a GET request
note: Public verb shortcuts (GET/POST/...) + Handle live in routergroup.go — the idiomatic registration API.

query: how do I create a new gin engine / router instance
note: New() and Default() constructors live in gin.go — the canonical setup entry points.

query: how do I return a JSON response from a handler
note: c.JSON()/c.IndentedJSON() — the user-facing render call — are methods on Context in context.go. (render/json.go is the impl, not how a user invokes it.)

query: how do I attach middleware to a router group
note: RouterGroup.Use(...) is the idiomatic middleware-attach API. Single gold.

query: how do I switch gin between debug and release mode
note: SetMode / gin.ReleaseMode constants live in mode.go — the idiomatic mode-switch API.
